data.text:
Build a polished, responsive YouTube-style video platform.

Use the available Java backends:
- auth-service for register, login, logout, and current-user state
- tweeter-service for video posts/channels; treat following a creator as subscribing
- media-service upload intents for attaching videos and thumbnails to posts
- comment-service for video comments
- post-search-service for searching videos

Include:
- dark YouTube-style home feed
- sidebar navigation
- video cards with thumbnails, creator, views, and timestamps
- video detail/watch page
- register/login modal
- upload-video workflow with title, description, video, and thumbnail
- comments section
- Subscribe/Unsubscribe button backed by follow/unfollow
- search bar backed by post search
- loading, empty, success, and visible error states
- responsive mobile layout

Use only endpoints listed in the workspace plug skill. Do not invent APIs. Mark unsupported features such as real view counts or recommendations as “Being developed — backend not available yet.” Run verify-backend.sh before finishing.

Built a single-file static app in [index.html](/home/navid/java/app-builder/agent-backend/workspaces/build-a-polished-responsive-yout-8331/index.html) with a dark YouTube-style layout: BFF-backed home/watch views, sidebar/mobile nav, auth modal, post-search-backed search, comment flow, subscribe/unsubscribe via follow endpoints, and an upload workflow that creates a post then uses `media-service` upload intents for video and thumbnail attachments before indexing search. Unsupported areas like real view counts and recommendations are rendered as visible “Being developed — backend not available yet” placeholders.

`./verify-backend.sh` did not pass in this environment. The real result was `curl: (7) failed to open socket: Operation not permitted` during the first CORS preflight call to `http://localhost:18000`, so backend wiring could not be confirmed from this sandbox.
